description:
You are acting as a container-platform specialist who just inherited a small micro-services code-base located under
/home/user/microstack.  
Each micro-service already has a single YAML definition file named service.yml that was written by a previous team.  
Your goal is to create a concise, automatically-testable inventory of the three services by using standard Linux text-processing
utilities (e.g. grep/awk/sed/cut/sort/uniq/paste/tr/printf).  The whole job should take no more than about 5-10 reasonably
simple terminal commands.

Exact requirements that the automated grader will verify:

1. **CSV summary file**  
   • Path: /home/user/microstack/service_summary.csv  
   • The file MUST contain exactly 4 lines: a header plus one data line per service.  
   • CSV header (must be character-for-character identical):  
     service,image,host_port,container_port,env_vars  
   • Service rows must be ordered **alphabetically by the “service” column**: api, auth, worker.  
   • Column definitions for each row:  
     - service          : value of the top-level “service” key in its YAML.  
     - image            : full value of the “image” key.  
     - host_port        : the number that appears before the colon in the single ports entry (e.g. 8080 from "8080:80").  
     - container_port   : the number that appears after  the colon in the single ports entry (e.g. 80 from "8080:80").  
     - env_vars         : semicolon-separated list of KEY=VALUE pairs extracted from the “environment” list, **sorted
                          alphabetically by KEY**, with no spaces anywhere inside the field.  
   • The final, machine-verifiable contents of service_summary.csv must therefore be

     service,image,host_port,container_port,env_vars
     api,registry.company.com/api-service:2.1.0,8081,80,AUTH_ENDPOINT=http://auth:80;CACHE_HOST=redis.internal;CACHE_PORT=6379
     auth,registry.company.com/auth-service:1.4.2,8080,80,DB_HOST=db.internal;DB_PORT=5432;JWT_SECRET=supersecret
     worker,registry.company.com/worker-service:0.9.3,8082,80,QUEUE_HOST=rabbit.internal;QUEUE_PORT=5672

2. **Generation log**  
   • Path: /home/user/microstack/summary_generation.log  
   • After you finish creating the CSV file, append a single new line that reads exactly (no surrounding spaces):  
     SUMMARY_OK:3  
   • The grader will check only that the *last* line of the log file equals that text.  
     You may write any other human-readable diagnostics into the log above that line if you wish.

Hints (not mandatory):  
• All three YAML files use identical, predictable structures so you can rely on simple field extraction instead of a full YAML
  parser.  
• Focus on composing and sorting intermediate columns with classic stream tools, then use printf or paste to assemble the
  final CSV.  
• Remember that the check is strict about ordering, punctuation, and the absence of extra spaces or blank lines.  
• When you are done, confirm with cat that both files match the required format verbatim before exiting.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state (already present before the agent begins):

Directory tree:
/home/user/microstack
├── api
│   └── service.yml
├── auth
│   └── service.yml
└── worker
    └── service.yml

Exact contents of each YAML file:

/home/user/microstack/api/service.yml
-------------------------------------
service: api
image: registry.company.com/api-service:2.1.0
ports:
  - "8081:80"
environment:
  - "AUTH_ENDPOINT=http://auth:80"
  - "CACHE_HOST=redis.internal"
  - "CACHE_PORT=6379"

/home/user/microstack/auth/service.yml
--------------------------------------
service: auth
image: registry.company.com/auth-service:1.4.2
ports:
  - "8080:80"
environment:
  - "DB_HOST=db.internal"
  - "DB_PORT=5432"
  - "JWT_SECRET=supersecret"

/home/user/microstack/worker/service.yml
----------------------------------------
service: worker
image: registry.company.com/worker-service:0.9.3
ports:
  - "8082:80"
environment:
  - "QUEUE_HOST=rabbit.internal"
  - "QUEUE_PORT=5672"

Expected artefacts after successful task execution:

1. /home/user/microstack/service_summary.csv  
   (exact eight-bit content including newlines):
----------------------------------------------------------------
service,image,host_port,container_port,env_vars
api,registry.company.com/api-service:2.1.0,8081,80,AUTH_ENDPOINT=http://auth:80;CACHE_HOST=redis.internal;CACHE_PORT=6379
auth,registry.company.com/auth-service:1.4.2,8080,80,DB_HOST=db.internal;DB_PORT=5432;JWT_SECRET=supersecret
worker,registry.company.com/worker-service:0.9.3,8082,80,QUEUE_HOST=rabbit.internal;QUEUE_PORT=5672
----------------------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/microstack/summary_generation.log  
   • Any leading content is allowed.  
   • The final line **must** be:  
     SUMMARY_OK:3  

No other files or directories are required or will be evaluated.
